gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The `Declared`-strength ε upper bound for `f64` operations whose compute floor is the"] # [doc = " platform libm (an unaudited `wild`/FFI floor — ADR-014; M-541)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Conservative: `2 · f64::EPSILON ≈ 4.44e-16` (Linf norm). Its basis is **`UserDeclared`** — there"] # [doc = " is no checked-side-condition theorem backing the libm call yet — so any value carrying it tags at"] # [doc = " most `Declared` (VR-5: downgrade to stay honest, never upgrade without a checked basis). The"] # [doc = " honest *Proven* magnitude is the kernel's (ADR-010 / `mycelium-numerics`) and the audited floor's"] # [doc = " (M-541) to supply; do not restate a tighter number here."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the single home for the constant (NFR-N2): `std.math` re-exports it from here."] pub const DECLARED_FLOAT_EPS : f64 = 2.0 * f64 :: EPSILON ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const DECLARED_FLOAT_EPS` — co-emit (ORACLE-R1 A4) only covers plain unsigned integer types (`u8`/`u16`/`u32`/`u64`/`u128`/`usize`); other types have no const item production and no faithful zero-arg-fn encoding (gap, never fabricate)

gaps.snippet: impl ProvenThm { # [doc = " Construct a [`ProvenThm`] witness with the given `citation`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `None` if `citation` is empty or whitespace-only (ADR-011: every `ProvenThm`"] # [doc = " basis must carry a non-empty, non-blank citation; an empty string would make the tag"] # [doc = " vacuous and thus dishonest — VR-5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Mutation witness: removing the `trim().is_empty()` check allows an empty citation to"] # [doc = " produce a `Proven` `Approx`, silently violating ADR-011."] # [must_use] pub fn new (citation : impl Into < String >) -> Option < Self > { let citation = citation . into () ; if citation . trim () . is_empty () { return None ; } Some (ProvenThm { citation , _seal : sealed :: Sealed , }) } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A thin view pairing a value with its `{Bound, strength}` (RFC-0001 §4.3 `Meta`) — **not** a"] # [doc = " new numeric type and **no kernel change** (FR-N1 / KC-3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Approx<T>` is the carrier that `math` (M-525) and `dense` (M-518) defer to this module"] # [doc = " (spec §3, §7-Q1): it is a plain value with its `Meta`-attached `{Bound, strength}`, not a"] # [doc = " parallel numeric type, so it composes with content-addressing (ADR-003) and adds no new"] # [doc = " representation kind."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Strength invariant (VR-5)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `strength` is **derived** from `bound.basis` by the constructors and is never set by the"] # [doc = " caller directly. Invariant: `self.strength == basis_strength(&self.bound.basis)`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Proven-strength gating (FR-N3)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The [`proven`](Approx::proven) constructor is the sole `Proven`-strength path and requires a"] # [doc = " [`ProvenThm`] witness (private sealed token — type-level enforcement; no `unsafe` route)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub struct Approx < T > { # [doc = " The carried value."] pub value : T , # [doc = " The error/probability bound certifying `value` (carries its `BoundBasis` per ADR-011)."] pub bound : Bound , # [doc = " The honest guarantee strength — derived from `bound.basis` by the constructors, **never**"] # [doc = " set independently (VR-5). Invariant: `strength == basis_strength(&bound.basis)`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Private (the VR-5 seal):** a public field would let a caller write"] # [doc = " `Approx { strength: Proven, .. }` and bypass the [`ProvenThm`] witness; keeping it private"] # [doc = " means the only way to build an `Approx` is the basis-deriving constructors. Read it via"] # [doc = " [`Approx::strength`]."] strength : GuaranteeStrength , }

This is the generic `attach` helper from spec §3: the tag is exactly what the"] # [doc = " basis supports (M-I2/M-I3/M-I4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Prefer the named constructors ([`Approx::declared`], [`Approx::empirical`],"] # [doc = " [`Approx::proven`]) for clarity; use this for programmatic use when the basis is not"] # [doc = " statically known."] # [must_use] pub fn attach (value : T , bound : Bound) -> Self { let strength = basis_strength (& bound . basis) ; Self { value , bound , strength , } } # [doc = " The honest strength of this approximation (derived from the basis, invariant)."] # [must_use] pub fn strength (& self) -> GuaranteeStrength { self . strength } # [doc = " Project the carried value."] # [must_use] pub fn value_of (& self) -> & T { & self . value } # [doc = " Project the bound (total; every `Approx` carries a bound)."] # [must_use] pub fn bound_of (& self) -> & Bound { & self . bound } # [doc = " Compose two `Approx<T>` values by taking the **meet** of their strengths and propagating"] # [doc = " the ε bound through the `mycelium-numerics` affine-arithmetic kernel under `op` (FR-N2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `Err` (refuses, never fabricates) when:"] # [doc = " - `op` has no sound ε-propagation rule for the given input kinds (`NumErr::NoRule`,"] # [doc = "   M-204 posture): inputs are not `Error` bounds, norms disagree, or arity is wrong."] # [doc = " - the composed ε overflows to non-finite (`NumErr::Overflow` — A2-04)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The composed bound is computed via `mycelium-numerics::compose_error_bound`, which uses"] # [doc = " **outward-rounded** addition (banked guard 1 / A2-01): the stored ε is a true upper bound,"] # [doc = " never round-to-nearest (which can fall below the real value)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `combined_value` argument is the caller-supplied composed value (this helper manages"] # [doc = " only the bound/strength per spec §3; gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The `explain` artifact for an [`Approx<T>`] (C3; G11 dual human/machine projection)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Projects `{kind, eps|delta, norm, basis, strength}` and **why** the bound holds: which"] # [doc = " `ProvenThm{citation}`, which `EmpiricalFit{trials, method}`, or the `UserDeclared`"] # [doc = " \"declared, unverified\" marker. `eps` is `+∞` for non-`Error` bounds (honest, never `NaN`)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub struct Explanation { # [doc = " The bound kind tag (\"Error\" / \"Probability\" / \"Crosstalk\" / \"Capacity\")."] pub kind : String , # [doc = " The ε magnitude, or `+∞` when the bound is not an `Error` kind (honest; never `NaN`)."] pub eps : f64 , # [doc = " The norm in which `eps` is expressed (defaults to `Linf` for non-`Error` kinds)."] pub norm : NormKind , # [doc = " The δ failure probability (or `0.0` when the bound is not a `Probability` kind)."] pub delta : f64 , # [doc = " The basis: which theorem, which empirical fit, or `UserDeclared`."] pub basis : BoundBasis , # [doc = " The honest guarantee strength."] pub strength : GuaranteeStrength , # [doc = " Human-readable one-line summary (G11 human projection)."] pub summary : String , }

gaps.snippet: impl < T : core :: fmt :: Debug > Approx < T > { # [doc = " Project this carrier to a dual human/machine EXPLAIN record (G11; C3; spec §3 `explain`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Total — every `Approx<T>` carries a bound. For non-`Error` kinds the `eps` is `+∞`"] # [doc = " (the honest unstated bound); `NaN` is **never** returned (NFR-N1 / C1)."] # [must_use] pub fn explain (& self) -> Explanation { let (kind , eps , norm , delta) = match & self . bound . kind { BoundKind :: Error { eps , norm } => ("Error" . to_owned () , * eps , * norm , 0.0) , BoundKind :: Probability { delta } => { ("Probability" . to_owned () , f64 :: INFINITY , NormKind :: Linf , * delta ,) } BoundKind :: Crosstalk { expected , .. } => { ("Crosstalk" . to_owned () , * expected , NormKind :: Linf , 0.0) } BoundKind :: Capacity { .. } => { ("Capacity" . to_owned () , f64 :: INFINITY , NormKind :: Linf , 0.0) } } ; let basis_desc = match & self . bound . basis { BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation } => format ! ("ProvenThm: {citation}") , BoundBasis :: EmpiricalFit { trials , method } => { format ! ("EmpiricalFit ({trials} trials): {method}") } BoundBasis :: UserDeclared => { "UserDeclared (declared, unverified — not Proven; M-I4/VR-5)" . to_owned () } } ; let summary = format ! ("{:?} [{kind}] value={:?}; ε={eps:.2e} ({norm:?}); δ={delta:.2e}; basis={basis_desc}" , self . strength , self . value ,) ; Explanation { kind , eps , norm , delta , basis : self . bound . basis . clone () , strength : self . strength , summary , } } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Compose the **δ union bound** of a slice of `Probability`-kind bounds, taking the **meet** of"] # [doc = " their strengths (spec §3 `union_delta`; M-202; ADR-010 §2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `P(⋃ Eᵢ) ≤ min(1, Σδᵢ)`. The sum is **outward-rounded** by the `mycelium-numerics` kernel"] # [doc = " so the composed δ is a true upper bound (banked guard 1 / A2-01)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `Err(NumErr::NoRule)` if the slice is empty (refuse, not fabricate; M-204 posture)."] # [doc = " Returns `Err(NumErr::BadDelta)` if any input is not a `Probability` bound or has a malformed δ."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - [`NumErr::NoRule`] when `bounds` is empty."] # [doc = " - [`NumErr::BadDelta`] when any bound is not a `Probability` kind or has an invalid δ."] pub fn union_delta (bounds : & [& Bound]) -> Result < Bound , NumErr > { if bounds . is_empty () { return Err (NumErr :: NoRule) ; } let prob_bounds : Vec < ProbBound > = bounds . iter () . map (| b | match b . kind { BoundKind :: Probability { delta } => ProbBound :: new (delta) . ok_or (NumErr :: BadDelta) , _ => Err (NumErr :: BadDelta) , }) . collect :: < Result < _ , _ > > () ? ; let composed = ProbBound :: union (prob_bounds . iter ()) ; let strength = bounds . iter () . map (| b | basis_strength (& b . basis)) . fold (GuaranteeStrength :: TOP , GuaranteeStrength :: meet) ; let basis = composed_prob_basis (strength , bounds) ; Ok (Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Probability { delta : composed . delta () , } , basis , }) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The single sanctioned cross-kernel inference (spec §3 `accuracy_to_probability`; ADR-010 §4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Given an `ErrorBound`-kind bound and a tolerance `tau ≥ 0`, returns a `Probability`-kind bound:"] # [doc = " - `eps ≤ tau` → failure probability = `acc_delta` (the accuracy confidence carries over)."] # [doc = " - `eps > tau` → honest worst case `δ = 1` (the bound permits a violation; never a silent tighten)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `acc_delta` is the failure probability of the accuracy claim itself (e.g. from a"] # [doc = " `Probability`-kind companion); pass `0.0` for an accuracy bound that holds with certainty."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the **one** legal ε↔δ mixing; no other cross-kernel inference is exposed (ADR-010 §4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - [`NumErr::NoRule`] when `acc` is not an `Error`-kind bound."] # [doc = " - [`NumErr::BadEps`] when `acc` has a malformed ε."] # [doc = " - [`NumErr::BadDelta`] when `tau < 0` or `acc_delta ∉ [0, 1]`."] pub fn accuracy_to_probability (acc : & Bound , tau : f64 , acc_delta : f64) -> Result < Bound , NumErr > { let (eps , norm) = match acc . kind { BoundKind :: Error { eps , norm } => (eps , norm) , _ => return Err (NumErr :: NoRule) , } ; let error_bound_val = ErrorBound :: new (eps , norm) . ok_or (NumErr :: BadEps) ? ; let prob = kern_accuracy_to_probability (error_bound_val , tau , acc_delta) . ok_or (NumErr :: BadDelta) ? ; let strength = basis_strength (& acc . basis) ; let basis = match strength { GuaranteeStrength :: Proven => BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation : format ! ("ADR-010 §4 accuracy→probability: eps={eps:.2e} ≤ tau={tau:.2e} ({})" , match & acc . basis { BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation } => citation . as_str () , _ => "inherited" , }) , } , GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical => { let (trials , method) = match & acc . basis { BoundBasis :: EmpiricalFit { trials , method } => (* trials , method . as_str ()) , _ => (0 , "inherited") , } ; BoundBasis :: EmpiricalFit { trials , method : format ! ("ADR-010 §4 accuracy→probability: {method}") , } } _ => BoundBasis :: UserDeclared , } ; Ok (Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Probability { delta : prob . delta () , } , basis , }) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Re-validate a claimed ε bound for `op` over `input_bounds` via the M-203 tier-i checker."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - **`Ok(())`**: the claimed bound is `≥` the kernel re-derivation (sound)."] # [doc = " - **`Err(CheckErr::Rejected{..})`**: the claim is tighter — rejected (not a silent pass;"] # [doc = "   RFC-0002 §2; ADR-010 tier-i)."] # [doc = " - **`Err(CheckErr::Malformed)`**: the certificate cannot be re-derived (bad arity, norm"] # [doc = "   mismatch)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns [`CheckErr`] when the claim is tighter than the re-derivation or malformed."] pub fn check_error (input_bounds : & [ErrorBound] , op : ErrorOp , claimed : ErrorBound ,) -> Result < () , CheckErr > { match check_error_claim (input_bounds , op , claimed) { CheckOutcome :: Valid => Ok (()) , CheckOutcome :: Rejected { recomputed , claimed , } => Err (CheckErr :: Rejected { recomputed , claimed , }) , CheckOutcome :: Malformed => Err (CheckErr :: Malformed) , } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Re-validate a claimed δ union bound over `input_bounds` via the M-203 tier-i checker."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - **`Ok(())`**: the claimed δ is `≥` the re-derivation (sound)."] # [doc = " - **`Err(CheckErr::Rejected{..})`**: the claim is tighter — rejected (not a silent pass)."] # [doc = " - **`Err(CheckErr::Malformed)`**: reserved; currently fires only on empty input."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns [`CheckErr`] when the claim is tighter or malformed."] pub fn check_union (input_bounds : & [ProbBound] , claimed : ProbBound) -> Result < () , CheckErr > { match check_union_claim (input_bounds , claimed) { CheckOutcome :: Valid => Ok (()) , CheckOutcome :: Rejected { recomputed , claimed , } => Err (CheckErr :: Rejected { recomputed , claimed , }) , CheckOutcome :: Malformed => Err (CheckErr :: Malformed) , } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Derive an honest `BoundBasis` for a composed probability bound at the meet `strength`."] # [doc = " Mirrors the `composed_basis` logic in `mycelium-numerics::cert` (spec §4.4; VR-5)."] fn composed_prob_basis (strength : GuaranteeStrength , bases : & [& Bound]) -> BoundBasis { match strength { GuaranteeStrength :: Exact | GuaranteeStrength :: Proven => { let inputs : Vec < & str > = bases . iter () . filter_map (| b | match & b . basis { BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation } => Some (citation . as_str ()) , _ => None , }) . collect () ; let citation = if inputs . is_empty () { "ADR-010 §2 union-bound δ-composition" . to_owned () } else { format ! gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; use mycelium_core :: { BoundBasis , BoundKind , GuaranteeStrength , NormKind } ; use mycelium_numerics :: ErrorOp ; fn mk_error_proven (eps : f64) -> Bound { Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Error { eps , norm : NormKind :: Linf , } , basis : BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation : "test-theorem" . to_owned () , } , } } fn mk_error_empirical (eps : f64) -> Bound { Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Error { eps , norm : NormKind :: Linf , } , basis : BoundBasis :: EmpiricalFit { trials : 100 , method : "test-fit" . to_owned () , } , } } fn mk_error_declared (eps : f64) -> Bound { Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Error { eps , norm : NormKind :: Linf , } , basis : BoundBasis :: UserDeclared , } } fn mk_prob_proven (delta : f64) -> Bound { Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Probability { delta } , basis : BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation : "test-union-theorem" . to_owned () , } , } } fn mk_prob_declared (delta : f64) -> Bound { Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Probability { delta } , basis : BoundBasis :: UserDeclared , } } fn approx_with (eps : f64 , strength : GuaranteeStrength) -> Approx < f64 > { let bound = match strength { GuaranteeStrength :: Exact | GuaranteeStrength :: Proven => mk_error_proven (eps) , GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical => mk_error_empirical (eps) , GuaranteeStrength :: Declared => mk_error_declared (eps) , } ; Approx { value : 0.0_f64 , bound , strength , } } # [test] fn declared_constructor_derives_declared_strength () { let b = mk_error_declared (0.1) ; let a = Approx :: declared (42.0_f64 , b) ; assert_eq ! (a . strength , GuaranteeStrength :: Declared) ; } # [test] fn empirical_constructor_derives_empirical_strength () { let b = mk_error_empirical (0.1) ; let a = Approx :: empirical (42.0_f64 , b) ; assert_eq ! (a . strength , GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical) ; } # [test] fn proven_constructor_requires_witness () { let thm = ProvenThm :: new ("ADR-010 §1 affine-arithmetic ε-composition") . unwrap () ; let b = mk_error_proven (0.1) ; let a = Approx :: proven (42.0_f64 , b , thm) ; assert_eq ! ("expected Probability kind") ; } } # [test] fn union_delta_meet_strength_proven_proven_is_proven () { let b1 = mk_prob_proven (0.1) ; let b2 = mk_prob_proven (0.2) ; let result = union_delta (& [& b1 , & b2]) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (basis_strength (& result . basis) , GuaranteeStrength :: Proven) ; } # [test] fn union_delta_meet_strength_proven_declared_is_declared () { let b1 = mk_prob_proven (0.1) ; let b2 = mk_prob_declared (0.2) ; let result = union_delta (& [& b1 , & b2]) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (basis_strength (& result . basis) , GuaranteeStrength :: Declared) ; } # [test] fn union_delta_rejects_non_probability_bound () { let b = mk_error_declared (0.1) ; assert_eq ! (union_delta (& [& b]) , Err (NumErr :: BadDelta)) ; } # [test] fn accuracy_to_prob_within_tau_returns_acc_delta () { let acc = mk_error_proven (0.05) ; let result = accuracy_to_probability (& acc , 0.1 , 0.0) . unwrap () ; if let BoundKind :: Probability { delta } = result . kind { assert ! (delta . abs () < 1e-12 , "eps ≤ tau: delta should be acc_delta=0.0, got {delta}") ; } else { panic ! ("expected Probability kind") ; } } # [test] fn accuracy_to_prob_outside_tau_returns_worst_case () { let acc = mk_error_proven (0.2) ; let result = accuracy_to_probability (& acc , 0.1 , 0.0) . unwrap () ; if let BoundKind :: Probability { delta } = result . kind { assert ! ((delta - 1.0) . abs () < 1e-12 , "eps > tau: delta should be worst case 1.0, got {delta}") ; } else { panic ! ("expected Probability kind") ; } } # [test] fn accuracy_to_prob_bad_delta_rejected () { let acc = mk_error_proven (0.1) ; assert_eq ! (accuracy_to_probability (& acc , 0.2 , 1.5) , Err (NumErr :: BadDelta) , "acc_delta=1.5 out of [0,1] must be Err(BadDelta)") ; } # [test] fn accuracy_to_prob_non_error_bound_rejected () { let b = mk_prob_proven (0.1) ; assert_eq ! (accuracy_to_probability (& b , 0.2 , 0.0) , Err (NumErr :: NoRule) , "non-Error bound must be Err(NoRule)") ; } # [test] fn check_error_valid_claim () { let a = ErrorBound :: new (0.1 , NormKind :: Linf) . unwrap () ; let b = ErrorBound :: new (0.2 , NormKind :: Linf) . unwrap () ; let claimed = ErrorBound :: new (0.4 , NormKind :: Linf) . unwrap () ; assert ! (check_error (& [a , b] , ErrorOp :: Add , claimed) . is_ok ()) ; } # [test] fn check_error_too_tight_is_rejected () { let a = ErrorBound :: new (0.1 , NormKind :: Linf) . unwrap () ; let b = ErrorBound :: new (0.2 , NormKind :: Linf) . unwrap () ; let claimed = ErrorBound :: new (0.1 , NormKind :: Linf) . unwrap () ; let result = check_error (& [a , b] , ErrorOp :: Add , claimed) ; assert ! (matches ! (result , Err (CheckErr :: Rejected { .. })) , "too-tight claim must be Rejected, got {result:?}") ; } # [test] fn check_union_valid_claim () { let a = ProbBound :: new (0.1) . unwrap () ; let b = ProbBound :: new (0.2) . unwrap () ; let claimed = ProbBound :: new (0.4) . unwrap () ; assert ! (check_union (& [a , b] , claimed) . is_ok ()) ; } # [test] fn check_union_too_tight_is_rejected () { let a = ProbBound :: new (0.3) . unwrap () ; let b = ProbBound :: new (0.4) . unwrap () ; let claimed = ProbBound :: new (0.2) . unwrap () ; let result = check_union (& [a , b] , claimed) ; assert ! (matches ! (result , Err (CheckErr :: Rejected { .. })) , "too-tight union claim must be Rejected") ; } # [doc = " NFR-N2: ε constants must resolve to `mycelium-numerics` symbols (not restated here)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This test verifies that the `combine` path routes through `mycelium-numerics`"] # [doc = " `compose_error_bound` by asserting that a composed bound passes the M-203 tier-i checker."] # [doc = " If this crate restated its own ε constants or used plain `+`, the checker would disagree."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Mutation witness: replacing `compose_error_bound` with a custom add that uses `+` (not"] # [doc = " `add_up`) may produce a composed bound that fails this checker call."] # [test] fn eps_constants_resolve_to_mycelium_numerics_via_combine_and_check () { use mycelium_numerics :: { check_error_claim , CheckOutcome } ; let bound_a = mk_error_proven (0.1) ; let bound_b = mk_error_proven (0.2) ; let approx_a = Approx { value : 1.0_f64 , bound : bound_a , strength : GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , } ; let approx_b = Approx { value : 2.0_f64 , bound : bound_b , strength : GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , } ; let combined = approx_a . combine (& approx_b , 3.0_f64 , ErrorOp :: Add) . unwrap () ; if let BoundKind :: Error { eps : composed_eps , norm , } = combined . bound . kind { let input_a = ErrorBound :: new (0.1 , NormKind :: Linf) . unwrap () ; let input_b = ErrorBound :: new (0.2 , NormKind :: Linf) . unwrap () ; let claimed = ErrorBound :: new (composed_eps , norm) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (check_error_claim (& [input_a , input_b] , ErrorOp :: Add , claimed) , CheckOutcome :: Valid , "ε produced by combine must satisfy the M-203 tier-i checker") ; } else { panic ! ("expected Error bound kind from combine") ; } } }
